core_payload.content:
# N-Infinity Signal Fabric

## 1. Boundary
N-Infinity Signal Fabric owns the external lanes and operator-facing surfaces that feed work, urgency, and context into the swarm. This sub-hub exists so upstream signal sources are visible as their own boundary instead of being mixed together with the swarm's role roster and runtime substrate.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `a2c`, `deepmine`, `personal-ai-assistant`, `tracker`, and `human-ai-symbiosis`. Together they define the refinery input stream, mining supply, user-facing partner surface, execution-pressure signal, and human-governance contract that shape what the swarm should notice, prioritize, or defer.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own the runtime substrate that hosts jobs, the internal worker roster, or the abstract metrics that evaluate graph quality. Those belong to `runtime-fabric`, `swarm-roster`, and `governance-metrics`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: N-Infinity Signal Fabric groups the upstream lanes and user-facing surfaces that feed context, urgency, and supply into the swarm: A2C, DeepMine, Personal AI Assistant, Tracker, and Human-AI Symbiosis. It keeps incoming signals distinct from runtime substrate and worker roles. This matters because the swarm needs one visible boundary for intake pressure, operator context, and supply signals before those inputs are transformed into background execution, governance interpretation, role-specific action, or compounding work.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of n-infinity hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 26 outbound to ≤9.
